activation_results.reason: Input matches the description verbatim — canonical use case.
Verb swap from Audit to Review still clearly invokes the same scope and targets.
User explicitly names the skill kubernetes-manifest-audit.
SSH key forwarding on Windows is unrelated to Kubernetes manifest auditing.
Meta-question about skills generally — no Kubernetes audit intent.

criterion_results.text: Australian English used throughout the narrative (colour, optimise, behaviour, organise)
criterion_results.evidence: Narrative is short and uses no diagnostic spellings (no colour/optimise/behaviour/organise tokens present); cannot verify Australian English from the artefact.

criterion_results.text: Findings cite file:line evidence and are categorised by severity
criterion_results.evidence: Risk Register and Top risks cite paths like charts/api/templates/deployment.yaml:42 and group findings tag CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM.
Artefact includes Executive Summary, Workload Graph, Category Scores, Per-Group Findings, kube-bench results, Runtime Resilience Test, Risk Register, Action Batches, Suppressed.

judge_result.notes: The artefact demonstrates strong structural coverage with all expected canonical sections present, severity-tagged findings, and precise file:line evidence in the Risk Register. However, the narrative contains no Australian-English diagnostic tokens (no colour/optimise/behaviour/organise spellings appear), so the AusE criterion cannot be affirmatively verified from the artefact alone — hence a partial verdict.
